title: Installing Claude Code
subtitle: Get Claude Code running on your machine

sections.content:
Installing Claude Code is straightforward — it's an npm package, just like the ones you learned about in Level 7. You install it globally so it's available from any directory on your machine.

You'll need three things:

1. **Node.js** (which you already have from Level 7)
2. **The Claude Code package** (installed via npm)
3. **An Anthropic account** (for authentication)

Let's get set up.
keyPoints: Claude Code is installed via npm, just like other packages
It's installed globally (-g) so you can use it anywhere
You need an Anthropic account to authenticate

sections.instruction: Install Claude Code globally using npm. The `-g` flag makes it available system-wide, not just in one project.

troubleshooting.solution: Go back to Level 7, Lesson 5.1 to install Node.js first. npm comes bundled with Node.js.

sections.instruction: Verify the installation by checking the version. If you see a version number, Claude Code is installed correctly.
sections.expectedOutput: A version number like 1.x.x (any version number means it worked)

sections.question: What does the -g flag mean when installing an npm package?
sections.explanation: The -g flag installs the package globally, making it available as a command from any directory — not just one project.

sections.instruction: Now let's authenticate. Run `claude` to start it for the first time. It will walk you through signing in with your Anthropic account. Follow the prompts on screen — it will open a browser window for you to log in.

troubleshooting.solution: Make sure you have an Anthropic account. Visit console.anthropic.com to sign up.
Copy the URL shown in the terminal and paste it into your browser manually.

completionMessage: Claude Code is installed and authenticated! You're ready to start your first conversation with your AI pair programmer.
